title: My Faith Looks Up to Thee
author: Ray Palmer

scriptureRefs.note: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith
Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world

story: Ray Palmer, a 22-year-old teacher in New York City, wrote this hymn in 1830 while struggling with loneliness and doubt. He later met Lowell Mason on a street in Boston, and Mason asked if he had anything that could be set to music. Palmer showed him this poem, and Mason composed the tune OLIVET on the spot. After reading it, Mason told Palmer, 'Mr. Palmer, you may live many years and do many good things, but I think you will be best known to posterity as the author of these words.'

lines: My faith looks up to Thee,
Now hear me while I pray,
O let me from this day
As Thou hast died for me,
O may my love to Thee
While life's dark maze I tread,
